rubric_results.requirement: Module dependency DAG is acyclic and correctly directed
rubric_results.evidence: Verified imports in all core modules. models.py imports only stdlib (uuid, dataclasses, datetime, enum, typing). executors.py imports from models + llm_client only. engine.py imports from models, executors, events, hooks, store — never from server or agent. server.py imports from engine, models, store, config. agent.py imports from executors and models only — never from engine. store.py imports only from models. The DAG models → llm_client → executors → engine → server is strictly maintained with zero wrong-direction or circular imports.
rubric_results.file: src/stepwise/models.py:1-16, src/stepwise/executors.py:1-23, src/stepwise/engine.py:1-64, src/stepwise/server.py:1-37, src/stepwise/agent.py:1-27, src/stepwise/store.py:1-21

rubric_results.requirement: Error handling is consistent — no bare except clauses, errors logged and propagated
rubric_results.evidence: No bare 'except:' clauses in any library code (sole match is in a JSON template file). ~20 'except Exception' patterns found, all falling into defensible categories: (1) cancellation best-effort (engine.py:187,209,217,643 — swallowing cancel failures is standard practice for cleanup paths), (2) crash-to-error propagation with context (engine.py:1037→_handle_executor_crash, engine.py:2442→queue error event, engine.py:1256/1523→fail run with descriptive message), (3) non-critical background operations (cli.py version/upgrade checks, server_detect.py health probes, hooks.py:170 webhook failures logged with exc_info). The when-condition handler (engine.py:859, yaml_loader.py:109) properly logs warnings. Queue event loop (engine.py:2258) logs at error level with exc_info=True. Minor weakness: cancel-path swallowing could benefit from debug-level logging, but is not a defect pattern.

rubric_results.requirement: All dataclasses have complete to_dict()/from_dict() pairs
rubric_results.evidence: models.py contains 21 @dataclass definitions and 21 matching to_dict()/from_dict() pairs — complete 1:1 coverage. All model classes covered: ChainConfig (61/70), StepLimits (86/97), DecoratorRef (113/117), ExecutorRef (127/143), ExitRule (158/167), InputBinding (187/200), ForEachSpec (224/233), OutputFieldSpec (260/281), ConfigVar (309/328), FlowRequirement (358/371), StepDefinition (405/438), FlowMetadata (473/490), WorkflowDefinition (963/983), Sidecar (1014/1023), HandoffEnvelope (1040/1050), WatchSpec (1070/1081), SubJobDefinition (1099/1107), StepRun (1136/1156), JobConfig (1185/1193), Job (1224/1248), Event (1282/1293). The store layer consistently uses from_dict() for hydration (_row_to_job, _row_to_run) and to_dict() for persistence (save_job, save_run).

rubric_results.requirement: Tests cover happy path, error cases, and edge cases
rubric_results.evidence: 53 test files providing deep coverage. Sampled files demonstrate substantive testing beyond smoke tests: test_engine.py (56-80) verifies data propagation across multi-step DAGs with computed values. test_branching.py (28-66) tests conditional when-branching with mutual exclusion, verifying SKIPPED status on inactive paths. test_validation.py (39-80) tests stall detection for unbounded loops, missing catch-all warnings, and uncovered output combinations. test_executors.py (47-78) covers success, failure exit codes, non-JSON output, empty stdout, and input file passing. test_yaml_loader.py (16-80) covers expression evaluation depth: simple comparisons, string equality, attempt variables, compound expressions, any/all/sorted/len builtins, nested dict access. Additional dedicated files for edge cases: test_optional_inputs, test_for_each, test_concurrency, test_poll_executor, test_delegation, test_session_continuity.

rubric_results.requirement: User inputs validated at system boundaries (API, CLI, YAML)
rubric_results.evidence: API layer: Pydantic models (CreateJobRequest, FulfillWatchRequest, etc.) enforce type validation. Path traversal protection at server.py:465 via relative_to(). YAML parsing (yaml_loader.py) validates extensively: required fields, action enums (line 305-309), input binding format, exit rule semantics, prompt conflicts. Exit rule evaluation uses eval() with restricted __builtins__=SAFE_BUILTINS (yaml_loader.py:84-91). HOWEVER, a critical input validation gap exists at the shell execution boundary: engine.py:66-88 _interpolate_config() uses Template.safe_substitute() to inject raw, unsanitized input values into the 'command' config field. This interpolated string is then passed to subprocess.run(shell=True) at executors.py:229. Adversarial probe #6 confirmed full shell injection — semicolons and pipes in input values execute as shell commands. Additionally, step names accept emoji and RTL characters (adversarial probe #5) despite the documented kebab-case convention; no pattern enforcement exists in yaml_loader.py.

rubric_results.requirement: Database connections, file handles, and subprocesses properly cleaned up
rubric_results.evidence: SQLiteStore has explicit close() method (store.py:551-552). Server lifespan properly cancels all async tasks on shutdown — stream tasks, observer, stream monitor, engine task — with CancelledError handling, then calls store.close(). ScriptExecutor uses blocking subprocess.run() (executors.py:227), not Popen, so no orphaned processes. Agent cleanup uses process group signaling (agent.py). Zombie job recovery on server restart fails orphaned jobs from previous crashes. SQLite configured with WAL mode + busy_timeout=5000ms (store.py:41-43). ThreadSafeStore wraps connection with _LockedConnection (server.py:40-75). WebSocket dead connections pruned in _broadcast() (server.py:234-242). Minor gap: SQLiteStore lacks __enter__/__exit__ context manager, relying on explicit close() calls, but this is adequate for the usage pattern.

qualitative_summary: Code quality is strong for a pre-1.0 project. Module boundaries are clean with strict DAG enforcement and no circular imports. All 21 model dataclasses have complete to_dict()/from_dict() serialization pairs. Error handling follows consistent patterns — exceptions are either propagated with context, logged and recovered, or intentionally swallowed in best-effort cleanup paths. Test coverage is deep and substantive across 53 files, testing edge cases, error conditions, and complex DAG scenarios rather than just smoke tests. Resource cleanup is adequate with blocking subprocess calls, proper async task cancellation, and explicit store lifecycle management. The primary deficiency is input validation at the shell execution boundary: _interpolate_config() injects unsanitized user inputs into shell commands via Template.safe_substitute() before subprocess.run(shell=True), creating a confirmed command injection vulnerability (adversarial probe #6). This is the most critical gap for 1.0 readiness. The ThreadSafeStore race condition under concurrent load (per-call locking rather than per-transaction) is a related architectural concern that surfaced in adversarial testing but falls outside the code quality rubric scope.
